content:
The issue is that the INDEX&MATCH formulas are returning text values instead of numbers, which is causing the net exports calculation to fail.

Let me fix this by changing the INDEX&MATCH formulas to return the correct numeric values:

```json
{
  "analysis": "The INDEX&MATCH formulas are returning text values (country names, series codes, etc.) instead of the actual numeric data. This is because the formulas are referencing the wrong columns in the Data sheet.",
  "plan": "I'll update the INDEX&MATCH formulas to reference the correct columns in the Data sheet, which contain the numeric data.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook

file_path = 'gdp.xlsx'
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
task_ws = wb['Task']

# Update Exports formulas to reference correct column
print("Updating Exports formulas...")
for row in range(12, 18):
    for col in range(8, 13):
        col_letter = chr(64 + col)
        # Reference the correct column (I, J, K, or L) based on the year
        year_col = col - 7
        formula = f"=INDEX(Data!$I$21:$M$26,MATCH($D{row},Data!$B$21:$B$26,0),{year_col+1})"
        task_ws[f"{col_letter}{row}"].value = formula

# Update Imports formulas to reference correct column
print("\nUpdating Imports formulas...")
for row in range(19, 25):
    for col in range(8, 13):
        col_letter = chr(64 + col)
        year_col = col - 7
        formula = f"=INDEX(Data!$I$28:$M$33,MATCH($D{row},Data!$B$28:$B$33,0),{year_col+1})"
        task_ws[f"{col_letter}{row}"].value = formula

# Update GDP formulas to reference correct column
print("\nUpdating GDP formulas...")
for row in range(26, 32):
    for col in range(8, 13):
        col_letter = chr(64 + col)
        year_col = col - 7
        formula = f"=INDEX(Data!$I$35:$M$40,MATCH($D{row},Data!$B$35:$B$40,0),{year_col+1})"
        task_ws[f"{col_letter}{row}"].value = formula

wb.save(file_path)
print("\nFormulas updated!")

EOF
",
      "duration": 2.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
```
